projectDescription: A work-in-progress decompilation of Super Mario Galaxy 1 for the Nintendo Wii, reconstructing the original C++ source code from the game binary.
keyFeatures: Faithful C++ decompilation of Super Mario Galaxy 1 (Korean versions RMGK01 and RMGK02)
Build system using Python (configure.py) and Ninja for automated compilation and verification
Binary-matching verification with objdiff for per-function accuracy checking
Comprehensive symbol and split configuration (symbols.txt, splits.txt)
Coverage of all major game subsystems: gravity, camera, physics, animation, audio, AI, and rendering
CodeWarrior 3.0a3 compiler targeting PowerPC (Gekko) for accurate code generation
CI integration via GitHub Actions for automated build and decompilation progress tracking
Extensive game object hierarchy: LiveActor, NameObj, MapObj, AreaObj, Enemy, Boss, Player
publicApiSurface: LiveActor (base class for all interactive game objects)
NameObj (base object in the game's object hierarchy)
Nerve / Spine (state machine system for actor behavior)
configure.py (build system entry point: python configure.py)
splits.txt (file split definitions for decompiled translation units)
objdiff (binary diff tool for matching verification)
